title: Input Event Configuration Dialog Crashes Engine when Joypad Actions are selected
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.7.dev [2af7856]
Likely from commit a71749c8848762adf7c04eac7edef213183fbc6c


### System information

Godot v4.7.dev.mono (2af785641) - Arch Linux #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Thu, 19 Mar 2026 16:33:17 +0000 on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3060 Ti (nvidia) - AMD Ryzen 9 5900X 12-Core Processor (24 threads) - 31.25 GiB memory

### Issue description

Adding input events to an action will cause the engine to crash. If the configuration dialogue already has an event set and you try to set a joypad event.

### Steps to reproduce

Open project settings. 
Go To InputMap
Add an InputEvent to an action
Initially bind a keyboard event
Than bind a joypad event. (Or potentially any non keyboard event)
Will cause engine to crash

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

title: System theme detection broken on Windows
body:
### Tested versions

Not working in 4.7 dev 428cee4
Works correctly in 4.7 dev3
so it's something very recent

### System information

Windows 10

### Issue description

The editor settings `interface/theme/follow_system_theme` and `interface/theme/use_system_accent_color` don't work anymore.
When enabling system theme, the editor switches to Light theme (I have dark theme).
When enabling system accent color, the editor uses the default blue.

### Steps to reproduce

Enable aforementioned editor settings.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

title: [4.7 dev3] Trying to assign a mouse button + modifier key in Input Map crashes the editor
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in 4.7 dev 3
- Not reproducible in 4.7 dev 2

### System information

Godot v4.7.dev3 - ArchLinux SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC - Wayland display driver, Multi-window, 3 monitors - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX (RADV NAVI31) - AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor (16 threads) - 31.24 GiB memory

### Issue description

When in any project's Input Map editor, trying to assign a mouse button plus a modifier key (Ctrl, Alt, Shift) while hovering the LineInput crashes the engine.
This can be scrolling up/down, left/right click, or other buttons like Mouse4 or Mouse5.
This happens regardless of whether the key is actually pressed, or if the modifier and/or the key is manually ticked in the options.

### Steps to reproduce

Open any project
Go to Project Settings -> Input map
Add a new action
Add Event

Press any mouse button + a modifier key while hovering the LineInput
or
Select any mouse button in the list of available keys, then tick a modifier key below the LineInput.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

title: Enabling PointLight2D Shadow crashes Godot
body:
### Tested versions

v4.7.dev2.official [778cf54da]

### System information

Godot v4.7.dev2 - macOS Tahoe (26.3.1) - Single-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M1 (Apple7) - Apple M1 (8 threads) - 8.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

Enabling shadows of a PointLight2D crashes Godot.

### Steps to reproduce

Download the attached file. There is only one PointLight2D in an empty scene.
Click on the "PointLight2D / Light2D / Shadow".
Godot will crash.

It also crashes if it's left off and then enabled through code.

It does this every time.

If it's of any help, here is the log file that is generated by the crash:
…Actually I can't post it: "Field can not be longer than 65536 characters".

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[light-makes-it-crash.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26256787/light-makes-it-crash.zip)

title: Projects list has accessibility issues with screen readers after a number of projects are in it
body:
### Tested versions

4.6.1

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19044) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (Intel Corporation; 27.20.100.9664) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-1165G7 @ 2.80GHz (8 threads) - 39.84 GiB memory

### Issue description

When the projects list gets more than about 5-6 projects, it becomes impossible to navigate among them easily. The scroll bar for some reason is focusable and gets in the way and doesn't let me easily navigate up and down the list. This issue probably occurs with many other long list boxes.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Open the project manager with 5-10 projects (mine has 9) while a screen reader is active.
2. Try to navigate among all the projects. You will find that you can't go past the first or second, because focus will jump to the scroll bar.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

body: ### Tested versions - Reproducible in: 4.7.dev2 - Not reproducible in: 4.7.dev1, 4.6.2-rc2, v4.6.2.rc.custom_build [04b5fcf18] ### System information Godot v4.7.dev (792380565) - Windows 11 (build 26100) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1650 (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.9186) - AMD Ryzen 7 4800H with Radeon Graphics (16 threads) - 15.37 GiB memory ### Issue description Cannot display dual-column docks on the right side, and there are two unclickable buttons under Make Floating, as follow ### Steps to reproduce Open a new project, such as the official UI Accessibility sample project ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) [accessibility.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26220617/accessibility.zip)

title: Enabling external editor removes 'Text Editor' preferences category on next load
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible: 4.6.1 and 4.6.2-rc2
 

### System information

Godot v4.6.2.rc2 - Linux Mint 22.3 (Zena) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4080 Laptop GPU - 13th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i9-13900HX (32 threads) - 31.08 GiB memory

### Issue description

Turning on external editor support prevents disabling in the future. Manual editing of of the editor_settings tres file is required to restore the category. Once enabled, exited, and restarted, the entire Text Editor category is removed, preventing toggling of the option.


### Steps to reproduce

Enable external editor in the Text Editor category. I'm using emacsclient.

Exit.

Start Godot again. Category is missing from preferences screen.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

New project. Enable external editor in preferences. Exit. Restart.

I did not try from the launcher settings option.

title: GDExtension crash on macOS 26 (Tahoe): NSBundle bundleWithURL throws for non-bundle paths
`Variant` pools leak reporting can crash depending on static destruction order
body:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.6.1.stable (custom fork)

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.stable (N/A) - macOS Tahoe (26.3.1) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - Metal (Forward+) - integrated Apple M2 Pro (Apple8) - Apple M2 Pro (10 threads) - 32.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

As touched on in #106235 already, using `ERR_*` macros during shutdown is problematic due to static initialization/destruction order problems, that can seemingly start to appear somewhat randomly. One of those scenarios have manifested as a real crash for us, in our custom fork of 4.6.1-stable, as seen in this crash backrace:

```
std::__1::mutex::lock()
std::__1::unique_lock<std::__1::mutex>::unique_lock[abi:nn200100](std::__1::mutex&)
std::__1::unique_lock<std::__1::mutex>::unique_lock[abi:nn200100](std::__1::mutex&)
MutexLock<MutexImpl<std::__1::mutex>>::MutexLock(MutexImpl<std::__1::mutex> const&) (core/os/mutex.h:77)
MutexLock<MutexImpl<std::__1::mutex>>::MutexLock(MutexImpl<std::__1::mutex> const&) (core/os/mutex.h:77)
Object::assign_type_static(GDType**, char const*, GDType const*) (core/object/object.cpp:2436)
Object::get_gdtype_static() (core/object/object.h:868)
RefCounted::get_gdtype_static() (.cold.1) (core/object/ref_counted.h:37)
Object::get_gdtype_static() (core/object/object.h:867)
RefCounted::get_gdtype_static() (core/object/ref_counted.h:37)
ScriptBacktrace::get_gdtype_static() (core/object/script_backtrace.h:38)
ScriptBacktrace::get_class_static() (core/object/script_backtrace.h:38)
String const GodotTypeInfo::Internal::get_object_class_name_or_empty<ScriptBacktrace>() (core/variant/type_info.h:304)
TypedArray<ScriptBacktrace>::TypedArray() (core/variant/typed_array.h:56)
TypedArray<ScriptBacktrace>::TypedArray() (core/variant/typed_array.h:55)
CoreBind::OS::LoggerBind::log_error(char const*, char const*, int, char const*, char const*, bool, Logger::ErrorType, Vector<Ref<ScriptBacktrace>> const&) (core/core_bind.cpp:272)
CompositeLogger::log_error(char const*, char const*, int, char const*, char const*, bool, Logger::ErrorType, Vector<Ref<ScriptBacktrace>> const&) (core/io/logger.cpp:261)
_err_print_error(char const*, char const*, int, char const*, char const*, bool, ErrorHandlerType) (core/error/error_macros.cpp:117)
_err_print_error(char const*, char const*, int, String const&, bool, ErrorHandlerType) (core/error/error_macros.cpp:102)
PagedAllocator<Variant::Pools::BucketSmall, true, 4096u>::~PagedAllocator() (core/templates/paged_allocator.h:169)
__cxa_finalize_ranges
exit
-[GodotApplicationDelegate applicationWillTerminate:] (platform/macos/godot_application_delegate.mm:314)
__CFNOTIFICATIONCENTER_IS_CALLING_OUT_TO_AN_OBSERVER__
___CFXRegistrationPost_block_invoke
_CFXRegistrationPost
_CFXNotificationPost
-[NSNotificationCenter postNotificationName:object:userInfo:]
-[NSApplication terminate:]
OS_MacOS_NSApp::terminate() (platform/macos/os_macos.mm:1163)
invocation function for block in OS_MacOS_NSApp::start_main() (platform/macos/os_macos.mm:1129)
__CFRUNLOOP_IS_CALLING_OUT_TO_AN_OBSERVER_CALLBACK_FUNCTION__
__CFRunLoopDoObservers
__CFRunLoopRun
_CFRunLoopRunSpecificWithOptions
RunCurrentEventLoopInMode
ReceiveNextEventCommon
_BlockUntilNextEventMatchingListInMode
_DPSBlockUntilNextEventMatchingListInMode
_DPSNextEvent
-[NSApplication(NSEventRouting) _nextEventMatchingEventMask:untilDate:inMode:dequeue:]
-[NSApplication(NSEventRouting) nextEventMatchingMask:untilDate:inMode:dequeue:]
-[NSApplication run]
main (platform/macos/godot_main_macos.mm:133)
start
```

More specifically it's (in the `4.6` branch) this mutex that's already been destroyed by the time `PagedAllocator` tries to print its leak report:

https://github.com/godotengine/godot/blob/5bd79ab53ffe9952f371a10e31c17947cf9a176b/core/object/object.cpp#L2406-L2408

As far as I can tell there has been some refactoring to the top part of this backtrace in latest `master`, but the `static BinaryMutex` involved is still there, as are the static `Variant` pools and the leak reporting, so this should still be just as much of a problem.

### Steps to reproduce

N/A

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

title: Different behavior between single select and multi select in the scene tree
body:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: v4.5.2.stable.official [6ce3de25a], v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e]

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.stable - Windows 10 (build 19045) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Direct3D 12 (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3050 Ti Laptop GPU (NVIDIA; 32.0.15.8183) - AMD Ryzen 5 5600H with Radeon Graphics (12 threads) - 15.34 GiB memory

### Issue description

When selecting multiple instanced nodes and resetting a resource, the resource is getting removed instead of resetting to the default from the instanced scene.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0d6c1d06-1424-4e64-b741-eeac8671cd3e

This really makes an impact on usability when working with modular models. The temporary fix is to click on every node in the scene tree and reset the resource one node at a time.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Make a scene.
2. Instantiate that scene multiple times in an another scene.
3. Change some default resources on the instanced scene nodes.
4. Try resetting that resource when multiple nodes are selected.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[node_multiselect_difference_2026-03-23_22-27-37.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26193498/node_multiselect_difference_2026-03-23_22-27-37.zip)

title: Pressing buttons in floating embedded game editor lets you type in the script editor
body:
### Tested versions

v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e]

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.stable - Windows 11 (build 26200) - Multi-window, 1 monitor - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility)

### Issue description

Pressing the buttons on top of floating embedded game let's you type in the script editor which usually results in typing inputs into the script and messing it up and having to undo it.
Demonstration:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6c228fa4-f22b-49a9-882f-a22c5b3989e6

This happens because pressing the top buttons defocuses the game window (likely because the embedded window and the top buttons are from two separate windows), but I don't think it letting you interact with the current main screen (workspaces, script editor) is expected. Still I think it would be the most convenient if pressing any of the buttons simply immediatly refocused the window, or at least a warning was added when the game window is not in focus.

### Steps to reproduce

- Run project while the script editor is open.
- Suspend and unsuspend running project in the floating embedded game editor, don't manually refocus the game window.
- Start pressing any keys, it should start typing in the script editor.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

N/A

title: Godot ignores file permissions, overwrites read-only/protected files.
body:
### Tested versions

Tested on: v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6], v4.5.stable.official [876b29033], v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e], 

### System information

Godot v4.6.1.stable - Fedora Linux 43 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on Wayland - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 3 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Mesa Intel(R) Iris(R) Xe Graphics (TGL GT2) - 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-11370H @ 3.30GHz (8 threads) - 62.52 GiB memory

### Issue description

If a file is inside the `res://` directory and its permissions do not allow for modifications (through the read-only flag or user permissions), Godot will ignore it totally and act as if the file is normally writeable.

This is a problem for versioning of binary files (.scn, .res, as well as any imported ones like .fbx or .png) using Perforce or Git with LFS locking. Making it difficult if not precarious to work in teams on a project.

I'm not sure if the Godot Git plugin (https://github.com/godotengine/godot-git-plugin) addresses it. The last time I used it, it butchered my LFS setup. But regardless, it seems like the engine should respect read-only settings with no plugins.

Additional info:

- v4.4.1.stable.official [49a5bc7b6] and v4.5.stable.official [876b29033] seem to change the permissions of the file.
- v4.6.1.stable.official [14d19694e] seems to leave the file permissions as is, but still overwrites it.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create any file in the godot res:// folder (for example a material).
2. Change it to be read-only either through the file system or a VCS.
3. Try to modify it in Godot.
4. The file will be modified, regardless of its permissions.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[read-only.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26181293/read-only.zip)
